name: intervaltree (Python Mutable Interval Tree)

performance.notes: O(log n + m) query time; Python-based so slower than C alternatives for huge datasets

agent_readiness.notes: Mutable augmented interval tree for Python. from intervaltree import IntervalTree, Interval. t = IntervalTree(). t[1:5] = 'first'. t[3:8] = 'second'. t.add(Interval(6, 10, 'third')). t[4] → {Interval(1,5,'first'), Interval(3,8,'second')} (point query). t[3:6] → all overlapping intervals. t.remove(Interval(1,5,'first')). t.chop(2,4) → removes portions. t.slice(5) → split intervals at point. t.items() → all intervals. sorted(t). t.begin(), t.end(). Interval(begin, end, data). begin is inclusive, end is exclusive. Empty intervals not allowed. Used for: scheduling conflicts, text annotation spans, genomics ranges, timeline overlap detection.

gotchas: No type annotations — limited IDE support
Development activity slow — last major release 2019
End is exclusive (half-open intervals) — can surprise developers
Pure Python — not suitable for millions of intervals (use C-based alternatives)
best_when: Overlap detection between time ranges or numeric intervals
Text span annotation and overlap queries
Genomics or bioinformatics range queries at moderate scale
avoid_when: Millions of intervals — use ncls or sorted containers with bisect
When sortedcontainers + bisect is sufficient for simple cases
Immutable interval lookups (use a simpler binary search)
summary: intervaltree is a mutable Python interval tree. t = IntervalTree(); t[1:5] = 'data'; t[4] → overlapping intervals; t[3:6] → range overlap. chop(), slice() for editing. Interval(begin, end, data). Apache-2.0. Good for scheduling conflicts, text spans, genomics ranges. No type annotations. Pure Python — slow for millions of intervals. End is exclusive. Development slow since 2019.
